channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mitchell Kemboi: Close Friend Mourns and Shares Heartfelt Messages After Tragic Accident
item.description: Mitchell Kemboi, the daughter of Kenya Ports Authority boss Captain William Ruto, tragically lost her life in a road accident in Taita Taveta after their car collided with a stationary trailer. The in...

item.title: Assam CM's Allegations Against Congress and Other Key Political Developments
item.description: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has alleged that a Pakistani social media group supplied the documents which Congress used to accuse his wife of holding multiple foreign passports. He also cl...

item.title: Trump Proposes $1.5 Trillion Defense Budget with Cuts to Non-Defense Spending
item.description: US President Donald Trump has proposed a major increase in the country's defense budget for the 2027 fiscal year, suggesting a rise of $500 billion, bringing total defense spending to approximately $1...

item.title: Inside LG’s Rollable Prototype: Why Rollable Smartphones Never Reached the Market
item.description: A recent teardown of LG’s never-released Rollable smartphone reveals why rollable phones never became a mainstream reality. According to Ars Technica, the concept, though visually stunning and innovat...

item.title: How European institutions still influence the restitution of Africa’s cultural heritage
item.description: Theophilus Khumomotse’s opinion piece explores how Europe’s private institutions continue to exercise control over Africa’s cultural and human heritage long after colonial rule formally ended. He argu...

item.title: Family Claims Delhi Assembly Intruder Sarabjit Singh Is Mentally Unstable
item.description: The reported incident involves Sarabjit Singh, a man who allegedly breached the security of the Delhi Assembly. According to the details referenced in the unavailable NDTV article, Singh's unexpected ...

item.title: Three concertgoers hurt after safety fence collapses during Super Junior show in Seoul
item.description: During Super Junior’s concert in Seoul, South Korea, three fans sustained injuries after a safety fence in the audience area collapsed. According to initial reports, the incident happened while the gr...

item.title: Advance voting closes as crucial federal byelections near that could determine Liberal majority
item.description: Advance voting for three federal byelections in Canada ended on Monday, April 6, 2026. The contests are taking place in two Ontario ridings — Scarborough Southwest and University–Rosedale — and one in...

item.title: Commuters' Network gives free meals to help jeepney drivers affected by modernization issues
item.description: A commuters’ advocacy group in the Philippines has started a free meals program to support jeepney drivers who continue to face challenges due to the government’s public utility vehicle (PUV) moderniz...

item.title: Exploring the rise of barefoot hiking and its physical and sensory benefits
item.description: Barefoot hiking, once a fringe curiosity, is quietly gaining traction across diverse landscapes from South Korea to Australia. The practice invites participants to connect directly with the terrain, e...

item.title: ICE Agents in Canada Will Not Carry Firearms During FIFA World Cup
item.description: The U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has clarified that its agents stationed in Canada will not carry firearms during the upcoming FIFA World Cup, addressing concerns about a potential I...

item.title: Boda Boda Rider Struggles to Get Drunk Passenger Off His Motorcycle
item.description: A video of a boda boda rider dealing with a drunk passenger has gone viral, sparking both frustration and amusement online. The rider, who is visibly exasperated, repeatedly asks the young woman to ge...

item.title: Exploring 19th Century Visions of France in the Year 2000
item.description: In 1899, a series of postcards imagined what France would look like 100 years in the future. The designs are a fascinating mix of naive optimism and some surprisingly accurate predictions. These postc...

item.title: Three Indian-Inspired Ways to Turn Eggs into a Satisfying Main Meal
item.description: Australian food writer Sarina Kamini explores three inventive Indian-inspired methods for transforming humble eggs into hearty, flavour-packed main meals in her latest Good Food feature. She explains ...

item.title: Four dead and several injured in two taxi collisions in Mpumalanga over Easter weekend
item.description: Four people lost their lives and many were injured in two unrelated taxi crashes that took place in Mpumalanga on Monday, marking a sombre end to the Easter long weekend. The first collision happened ...

item.title: BFP removes all NCR fire inspectors as part of corruption cleanup effort
item.description: The Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) has ordered the relief of all fire inspectors assigned in the National Capital Region (NCR) as part of a wide-ranging effort to eliminate corruption within its rank...

item.title: Team Rivers Wins 53 Medals on Day 5 of Para Games, Director Applauds Athletes' Efforts
item.description: The Para Games in Rivers have seen the state's team shine brightly on day 5, winning a total of 53 medals in various sporting events. Despite being first-time participants and having a late start in p...

item.title: Ondo State Wrestlers Dominate Women’s Categories at Diri Classics
item.description: Ondo State female wrestlers really show dem muscle for Day Six of the Fourth Governor Douye Diri Wrestling Classics wey happen for Yenagoa on Monday. Dem capture two gold medals from the four women fr...

item.title: 12-Year-Old Arrested Following Fatal School Assault in Los Angeles
item.description: In Los Angeles, a 12-year-old student has been arrested in connection with the death of classmate Khimberly Zavaleta Chuquipa, who was struck in the head with a metal water bottle. Khimberly, who was ...

item.title: Mombasa restricts heavy trucks on Nyali Bridge during peak traffic hours
item.description: The County Government of Mombasa has announced temporary restrictions on heavy commercial vehicles using Nyali Bridge and other major Northern Mainland roads during peak hours, beginning next week unt...
